prose:
Method provenance – pyridine frontier-orbital gap
Property: HOMO/LUMO energies and HOMO–LUMO gap (eV)
Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (no density fitting) in PySCF 2.x (via chemkit)
Geometry provenance: single-point on the user-supplied pyridine.xyz (no re-optimization)
Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent)
Charge = 0, multiplicity = 1 (closed-shell singlet)
SCF convergence: converged in 10 cycles; integrity.status = ok, trustworthy = true; no warnings reported.
Live engine log: written during the run to the standard chemkit `frontier_YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS.out` file in the working directory; full result JSON saved as `pyridine_frontier_b3lyp_def2tzvp.json` (see cli_invocation path above) for reproduction.

Results (electron-volt):
  HOMO  = −7.2525992 eV
  LUMO  = −1.112786 eV
  HOMO–LUMO gap = 6.1398131 eV  ← headline value

No warnings were emitted by the engine. The calculation used chemkit’s frontier-orbitals skill with explicit flags `--method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp`; the result is a computed (not experimental) estimate of the Koopmans-style orbital gap for gas-phase pyridine.
